# Test: apx breaking with actual buf execution
# Verifies that buf breaking-change detection works end-to-end

# Setup: create a git repo with a canonical structure
mkdir breaking-test
cd breaking-test

exec git init
exec git config user.name 'Test User'
exec git config user.email 'test@example.com'

exec apx init canonical --org=testorg --repo=test-apis --skip-git --non-interactive

# buf.work.yaml must NOT be generated
! exists buf.work.yaml

# Create a valid proto file and commit
mkdir -p proto/test/v1
cp $WORK/valid.proto proto/test/v1/test.proto
exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Initial version'

# Create second commit (no schema change)
exec git commit --allow-empty -m 'Second commit'

# Breaking detection should succeed — no changes means no breakage
exec apx breaking --against=HEAD~1 proto

# Test with missing --against flag (should fail with helpful error)
! exec apx breaking proto
stderr '--against is required'

# Cleanup
cd ..
rm -rf breaking-test

-- valid.proto --
syntax = "proto3";

package test.v1;

message GetTestRequest {
  string id = 1;
}

message GetTestResponse {
  string result = 1;
}

service TestService {
  rpc GetTest(GetTestRequest) returns (GetTestResponse);
}
